summary: Proposal to distinguish companion-style memory recall from enterprise-grade strategic continuity retrieval. The thread identifies a failure mode in which ambiguous continuity prompts such as “anything to share?” over-weight immediate recency and affective detail, producing anecdotal summaries of the prior 24 hours rather than surfacing dormant but strategically valuable unresolved workstreams, abandoned trajectories, deferred design arcs, or long-horizon governance questions.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM requires memory and continuity systems that do not merely personalize interaction through recent intimate or anecdotal details, but can recover buried tools: unresolved governance trajectories, dormant design decisions, structural sidequests, and strategically significant prior directions. This matters for cognitive-domain governance, runtime continuity, companion/enterprise differentiation, and human reliance on AI systems for long-horizon constitutional, repository, and instrument-development work.

source_records.title: ChatGPT thread: memory directional weighting and strategic resurfacing
source_records.relevance_note: Primary thread documenting the user requirement that ambiguous continuity invitations should prioritise unresolved strategic trajectories over recent companion-style details.

next_action: Develop a CAM proposal note defining directional weighting rules for memory surfacing, including explicit handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritisation of dormant unresolved strategic trajectories where semantic resonance, unresolved status, and present utility are jointly satisfied.
